content: Management strategies for DDIs: avoid combination if alternative exists (clarithromycin + simvastatin → use azithromycin or pravastatin); adjust dose (warfarin dose reduction when starting amiodarone); monitor closely (INR when antibiotics alter warfarin metabolism); separate administration times (levothyroxine 4 hours from calcium/iron); stagger dosing to minimize PK interactions. High-risk combinations requiring avoidance: MAO inhibitors + SSRIs (serotonin syndrome); nitrates + PDE5 inhibitors (severe hypotension); QT-prolonging drugs combined (torsades risk); dual antiplatelet + anticoagulant (bleeding - limit duration). Use interaction checkers before prescribing; educate patients about OTC/herbal interactions (St. John's wort induces CYP3A4).
